Log10-transform the concentration-like columns of a PCA matrix

Source: R/impute.R
dot-log_transform_pca.Rd

Applies log10() to every column whose name is not in .PCA_NO_LOG_VARS, leaving pH / temperature / ORP / DO on their native scale. NA cells are preserved (so NIPALS can still handle within-sample missingness), and a small floor guards against log10(0) for genuine zeros. Both the training PCA (.prepare_chem_pca()) and the scoring projection (.compute_pca_scores()) call this so the transform is identical on both paths.

Usage

.log_transform_pca(mat, eps = 1e-09)

Arguments

mat

Numeric matrix with named columns (samples × variables).

eps

Positive floor applied before the log (default 1e-9).
